f64_certification_status: CERTIFIED (CPU-F32, ADR-004 primary metric) — CERTIFY-BREADTH-2. Atenia's CPU F32 forward vs a PyTorch F64 reference on the canonical 4-token sequence [1,100,200,300]: max_abs_diff_vs_f64 = 0.001249 (threshold 0.5, ~400x margin), per-position argmax match 4/4. Reproduce: (1) `python tests/fixtures/generate_f64_reference.py models/gemma-3-1b-it tests/fixtures/gemma3_1b_reference`; (2) `GEMMA3_1B_DIR=models/gemma-3-1b-it cargo test --test certify_breadth_f64_validation_test --release -- --ignored gemma3_1b_atenia_f32_matches_f64 --nocapture`. NOTE: this certifies the CPU-F32 graph-execution path (the same ADR-004 primary metric the Llama/Qwen M4.6 CPU tests use). The GPU-TF32 production kernel number (cuda_matmul_bf16_inplace) is a SEPARATE field still pending a CUDA run. Caveat: Gemma 3 alternates local-sliding-window (window 512) and global attention; the text builder ships full causal attention, mathematically equivalent only for contexts shorter than the window — the 4-token fixture is well within it, so long-context drift is a separate follow-up.
generated_on: F64 reference: PyTorch 2.5.1+cpu / transformers 5.6.2, Windows 11 (CPU). Atenia: CPU F32 forward (mmap loader), RTX 4070 Laptop host, 32 GB RAM.
config_compatibility_notes: Gemma 3 (text) extends the Gemma 2 deltas with per-head QK-Norm and dual-RoPE (global layers use rope_theta=1e6, local sliding-window layers use rope_local_base_freq=1e4, period sliding_window_pattern=6). The Gemma3ForCausalLM text path is validated functionally (MODEL_FAMILY_VALIDATION.md) AND now numerically CERTIFIED on the CPU-F32 path vs PyTorch F64 (CERTIFY-BREADTH-2).

certified_mode.kernel_path: CPU F32 graph execution (ADR-004 primary metric, via the certify_breadth harness — the same metric the Llama/Qwen M4.6 CPU tests use). The GPU-TF32 production kernel (cuda_matmul_bf16_inplace) number is NOT covered here; it needs a separate CUDA run.
certified_mode.validation_notes: Measured CERTIFY-BREADTH-2 (2026-06-03): Atenia CPU F32 vs PyTorch F64 on tokens [1,100,200,300] — max_abs_diff_vs_f64 = 0.001249 (~400x inside the 0.5 gate), per-position argmax match 4/4 (F64 argmax ids 161596 / 26503 / 240960 / 206500). No mean/rmse recorded — the harness reports max_abs_diff + argmax only; no number fabricated.

fast_mode.validation_notes: Fast-mode drift uncharacterised; functionally validated only (MODEL_FAMILY_VALIDATION.md).

recommended_mode_rationale: Certified mode is the measured, ADR-004-passing CPU-F32 path (max_abs_diff 0.001249 vs F64). Fast mode (GPU-TF32 / BF16-TC) drift is still uncharacterised for Gemma 3, so certified remains the recommended default.
per_tensor_policy_status: Reserved — fast-mode (GPU) drift is not yet characterised for Gemma 3, so no per-tensor fast routing is claimed.

verification.expected: max_abs_diff vs F64 = 0.001249 (< 0.5), per-position argmax match 4/4 (CPU-F32 / ADR-004 primary metric).
verification.note: Certifies the CPU-F32 graph path. The GPU-TF32 production kernel number is a separate, still-pending field.
